channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory
item.description:
This post will be a  lot less policy oriented than normal. That’s because as the head of RGF, this week has been VERY busy. It started with a HUGE win at ABQ City Council on Monday night when on a 5-4 vote Council (again) rejected a major tax increase. The vote was 5-4 against raising […]

The post RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Foropportunity] When a Love Story Meets the Tax Code
item.description:
Key Points If you ask many engaged couples what’s worrying them before the big day, you’ll probably hear about things like catering […]

The post When a Love Story Meets the Tax Code appeared first on For Opportunity.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Portland Study Reinforces What We Already Know: Inclusionary Zoning is Terrible Policy
item.description:
For years, Maine Policy Institute has warned that Portland’s inclusionary zoning policy would make the city’s housing shortage worse, not better. Last December, when Portland commissioned yet another study on the subject, MPI noted that the evidence was already clear: Inclusionary zoning acts like a tax on housing development, raises the cost of building, and […]

The post Portland Study Reinforces What We Already Know: Inclusionary Zoning is Terrible Policy appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Rifreedom] ONE SCORE, Twenty Failures: The High Cost of Rhode Island’s One-Party Rule
item.description:
Rhode Island's twenty year single-party rule has driven sharp decline. The new “One Score” report details twenty major policy failures in taxes, affordability, education, business climate & more. Read it now.

The post ONE SCORE, Twenty Failures: The High Cost of Rhode Island’s One-Party Rule appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Governor Sherrill is Targeting Junk Fees, but Where Do Government Fees Stand?
item.description:
by Megan Whitman NJPI Junior Policy Fellow   Governor Sherrill recently signed an Executive Order targeting “junk fees,” fees that […]

The post Governor Sherrill is Targeting Junk Fees, but Where Do Government Fees Stand? first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] We Can’t Afford Metro’s Affordable Housing
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Affordable housing projects funded with Metro’s 2018 housing bond have...

The post We Can’t Afford Metro’s Affordable Housing appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Laconia beats Boston to launch first social district in New England
item.description:
On March 21, Laconia launched New England’s first social district, a designated area where drinking is allowed in public under tightly regulated conditions.  With Motorcycle Week looming, critics on social media mockingly predicted disaster.  A week later, Joia Hughes, Laconia’s economic development and housing director, summed up the damage. “No problems,” she said. Mayor Mike […]

The post Laconia beats Boston to launch first social district in New England appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Empowerms] Amanda Delverdank is building something special in the Mississippi Delta
item.description:
Originally from Utah, Amanda came to Mississippi through Teach for America and began her journey in education as an art teacher in Greenville. Over time, her passion for children, community, and meaningful learning experiences continued to grow. While working in schools, she began noticing something powerful: children often learned best when they were allowed to…

The post Amanda Delverdank is building something special in the Mississippi Delta first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF on with Bob Clark for TWO fantastic interviews
item.description:
On Thursday, June 25, 2026 RGF hosted a luncheon with speaker Tim Sandefur as part of the organization’s “America 250” celebrations. Tim signed copies of his book: “Proclaiming Liberty: John Adams, Thomas Jefferson, and the Declaration of Independence.” You can check out the interview Bob Clark of KKOB radio did with Tim below: The following day Paul […]

The post RGF on with Bob Clark for TWO fantastic interviews appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Fourteen NYC Educators Receive Over Half a Million Dollars in Pensions
item.description:
Fourteen retired New York City educators received more than $500,000 in pension benefits in 2025, according to data released by....

The post Fourteen NYC Educators Receive Over Half a Million Dollars in Pensions appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Yankee Institute’s 2026 Public Policy Toolkit
item.description: At Yankee Institute, we envision a Connecticut that is affordable, livable, and workable. Our 2026 Public Policy Toolkit advances that vision by explaining how policy decisions function in practice, making […]The post Yankee Institute’s 2026 Public Policy Toolkit appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] What Matters Most to You as a Teacher?
item.description:
Teachers' unions are among the most politically active organizations in the country. They endorse candidates, contribute to political causes, and advocate for policies on issues far beyond wages and working conditions. They’ve taken positions on school choice, immigration, LGBTQ issues, curriculum, library books, and other controversial topics.

The post What Matters Most to You as a Teacher? appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America
item.description:
Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America As America approaches its 250th anniversary, what exactly are we celebrating? In this episode, Grant sits down with attorney, writer, and student of history Andy Taggart to explore the ideas behind the Declaration of Independence and why they remain relevant nearly two and a half centuries later. They…

The post Andy Taggart: The Ideas That Made America first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska’s Child Care Costs Are Holding Back Young Families
item.description:
We recently posted about childcare regulations, and Nebraska’s solid standing according to the Archbridge Institute. But there are lots of data points to consider when it comes to childcare issues, and how childcare influences a state’s workforce, and another one showed up today, from Wallethub–childcare costs. When we talk about Nebraska’s outmigration problem, we often...

The post Nebraska’s Child Care Costs Are Holding Back Young Families appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJPI: Largest Budget in State History Falls Short on Transparency and Fiscal Responsibility
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE   Parsippany, NJ – The New Jersey Policy Institute today expressed disappointment with the process surrounding the adoption […]

The post NJPI: Largest Budget in State History Falls Short on Transparency and Fiscal Responsibility first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Lamont Got the Endorsement. What Did Labor Get?
item.description: On June 26, Gov. Ned Lamont won the Connecticut AFL-CIO’s endorsement over Democratic primary challenger Josh Elliott following closed-door interviews with both candidates. The Connecticut AFL-CIO is the state’s largest […]The post Lamont Got the Endorsement. What Did Labor Get?  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Special Circumstance Reviews
item.description:
Special Circumstance Reviews Between 2023 and 2025, the West Virginia Board of Education launched nine Special Circumstance Reviews — structured state investigations into county school systems exhibiting signs of serious failure. These reviews, grounded in WV Code §18-2E-5 and WVBE Policy 2322, culminated in state takeovers of multiple districts. Taken together, they reveal not a […]

The post Special Circumstance Reviews appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Regulatory Reform in West Virginia
item.description:
Regulatory Reform in West Virginia The Problem: A State Buried in Red Tape West Virginia has accumulated one of the heaviest regulatory burdens in the nation. According to the State RegData project, the state now has 131,007 regulatory restrictions on the books — mandatory rules containing language like “shall,” “must,” and “may not” that impose […]

The post Regulatory Reform in West Virginia appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Cardinalinstitute] Charity Care
item.description:
Charity Care West Virginia non-profit hospitals enjoy billions in tax exemptions. In return, the social contract demands they provide meaningful charity care to struggling families. The data shows they are not holding up their end of the bargain. This brief examines the evidence — from IRS filings, Medicare cost reports, and federal subsidy data — […]

The post Charity Care appeared first on Cardinal Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Needs Workers to Build AI and Workers to Use It
item.description:
The demand for American workers who can build, power, and apply AI systems is outpacing supply.  This deficit...

The post Florida Needs Workers to Build AI and Workers to Use It appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Oregon’s largest teachers union prioritizing politics over education
item.description:
The Oregon Education Association has long claimed to speak for educators, students and public schools. But newly reported internal documents raise a fair question for Oregon teachers and education employees: Is OEA primarily focused on representing the workers who fund it or on growing its own political power?

The post Oregon’s largest teachers union prioritizing politics over education appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Record Month
item.description:
Every Fourth of July, Americans celebrate the ideals that founded this country — national sovereignty, individual liberty, self-determination and the right to make our own choices. At the Freedom Foundation, we see those principles in action every day. June was a record-breaking month for the organization, as 6,827 public employees exercised their rights and opted […]

The post Record Month appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] The Declaration at 250: The Radical Idea That Made America
item.description: Many Americans remember the Declaration as our breakup letter with Great Britain or simply as a rejection of monarchy. Yet it is far more than that.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Last Living Signer’s “Best Earthly Inheritance”
item.description:
On the 50th anniversary of America’s Independence, the Declaration’s last living signer, Charles...

The post The Last Living Signer’s “Best Earthly Inheritance” appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Why Josiah Bartlett held New Hampshire’s vote for independence to the last moment
item.description:
In 1776, Josiah Bartlett really could’ve used a good cell phone plan. Stuck in sweltering Philadelphia as July crawled ever closer on the calendar, the Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy’s namesake Founding Father was getting nervous.  New Hampshire’s lead delegate to the Continental Congress, Bartlett was a passionate patriot. His first year as a […]

The post Why Josiah Bartlett held New Hampshire’s vote for independence to the last moment appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Will politicians serve local government or their constituents on property tax reform?
item.description: A public opinion poll conducted last month shows that self-described conservatives, moderates, and liberals overwhelmingly want a constitutional limit on assessed valuations to protect them from unaffordable property tax hikes. No surprise there. The question is whether the candidates for the Kansas House of Representatives—to say nothing of other candidates or Senators not on the […]

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Record State Surplus Signals Need for Income Tax Relief
item.description:
Arkansas has now collected multiple record-breaking surpluses in recent years. It’s time to return this money to taxpayers by cutting the state income tax and giving working Arkansans a pay raise.

The post Record State Surplus Signals Need for Income Tax Relief first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Data centers could be a godsend — if communities let them
item.description:
A growing number of Wisconsin communities are choosing to act against considerable economic interest and sit out the data center revolution.

The post Data centers could be a godsend — if communities let them appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Herbert Hoover and the Declaration of Independence
item.description:
“The founders of our republic under Divine inspiration” established a “great political system,” which is “unique in the world,” argued Hoover. However, Hoover understood that the principles of the Declaration of Independence and the Constitution were also based upon heritage. “God has blessed us with another wonderful word—heritage,” affirmed Hoover. Further, Hoover noted that the “great documents of that heritage” were “the Bible, the Declaration of Independence, and the Constitution, within them alone can the safeguards of freedom survive.”

The post Herbert Hoover and the Declaration of Independence appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empowerms] Stewarding the American Dream
item.description:
As America approaches its 250th anniversary, I have found myself thinking less about politics and more about stewardship. Recently, Secretary of State Marco Rubio described America as “a story of perpetual improvement,” a nation where each generation leaves the next freer, safer, and more prosperous than the one before. That’s the essence of stewardship. The…

The post Stewarding the American Dream first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] API Applauds July 1st Implementation of Laws Empowering Mobile Businesses and Expanding Resources in K-12 Schools
item.description:
Alabama Policy Institute (API) is proud to announce the official implementation of three major legislative victories that dramatically cut regulatory red tape for entrepreneurs and restore foundational values to Alabama’s public education system.

The post API Applauds July 1st Implementation of Laws Empowering Mobile Businesses and Expanding Resources in K-12 Schools appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] U.S. Supreme Court Upholds Laws Protecting Women’s and Girls’ Sports
item.description:
Today, the Supreme Court of the United States issued a landmark ruling upholding Idaho’s Fairness in Women’s Sports Act and West Virginia’s Save Women’s Sports Act, affirming that states may protect female athletic opportunities by ensuring that eligibility for women’s and girls’ sports is based on sex consistent with Title IX.

The post U.S. Supreme Court Upholds Laws Protecting Women’s and Girls’ Sports appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Opportunityarkansas] Arkansas Voters Once Again Reject Attack on Education Freedom, Families, and Taxpayers
item.description:
Radical education amendment again fails to qualify for ballot

The post Arkansas Voters Once Again Reject Attack on Education Freedom, Families, and Taxpayers first appeared on Opportunity Arkansas.

item.title: [Beacontn] What’s the tea on the TEA—and teachers’ unions as a whole?
item.description: If you’re a teacher, do yourself a favor: keep your hard-earned dollars. Unions like the TEA aren’t brewed for you.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama: #1 Nationwide in Educational Savings Account Growth
item.description:
Governor Kay Ivey announced that the CHOOSE Act has been fully funded for every approved student for the upcoming school year; over 34,000 Alabama kids have unlocked the ability to exercise educational freedom. It is a huge step forward for our state and a victory that is unmatched when comparing programs from across the nation.

The post Alabama: #1 Nationwide in Educational Savings Account Growth appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s Electricity Prices Fourth Highest in the Country
item.description:
New York households are paying the fourth-highest electricity prices in the country, according to the Empire Center’s Energy Data Bulletin.....

The post New York’s Electricity Prices Fourth Highest in the Country appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] The Pros of AI and the Removal of CONs
item.description: In the same way that MRIs and CT scans were cutting-edge innovations that changed the game on early diagnosis and treatment, AI should be welcomed, not stifled by government red tape.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] How Lincoln Raised Property Taxes Without Raising Rates
item.description:
Lincoln’s new budget levies the same property tax rate as last year, with Mayor Gaylor Baird boasting that “except for voter-approved stormwater bonds, the city of Lincoln has not increased the property tax rate during my administration.” The statement is accurate—and yet Lincoln homeowners’ property tax bills will increase under the new budget. Same rate,...

The post How Lincoln Raised Property Taxes Without Raising Rates appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Empirecenter] The Attorney General’s MFCU SNAFU
item.description:
Attorney General Letitia James’ latest fight with the Trump administration focuses on New York’s Medicaid Fraud Control Unit, a federally....

The post The Attorney General’s MFCU SNAFU appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Affordability Requires More Energy, Not More Targets
item.description:
Governor Mikie Sherrill signed a package of energy affordability bills this week and announced $90 million in immediate relief for […]

The post Affordability Requires More Energy, Not More Targets first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Dismal Economics of Floating Offshore Wind
item.description:
There are at least three ways to evaluate the economic viability of energy projects. The easiest way is to just repeat whatever is in a press release. The other extreme, a mandatory exercise for anyone poised to actually invest and build a project, is to develop complex projections that take into account the benefits of...

The post The Dismal Economics of Floating Offshore Wind first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Bell-to-Bell Cell Phone Ban Goes Too Far
item.description:
By 2026 Maine Policy Institute Public Policy Intern Andrew Cook Maine’s new statewide ban on student use of cellphones in school is based on reasonable concerns: Cell phones and other electronic devices can be distracting in class, and teachers should not have to compete with group chats, games, or social media while trying to teach. […]

The post Maine’s Bell-to-Bell Cell Phone Ban Goes Too Far appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Sues Gov. Evers over $250 Million Discriminatory Program
item.description:
A Wisconsin-based taxpayer association, represented by WILL, filed a new lawsuit challenging the Wisconsin Supplier Diversity Program, a procurement program that spends over $250 million each year purchasing supplies and services based on race. 

The post WILL Sues Gov. Evers over $250 Million Discriminatory Program appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Star Power: Satellites in Space Boost Connectivity in Louisiana
item.description:
Louisiana has attacked the digital divide with grit and efficiency. The state’s broadband office, ConnectLA, has done well in prioritizing competition between providers and meeting requirements of the federal Broadband Equity Access and Deployment Program (BEAD) grant program since its announcement in 2021. As a result, Louisiana has led the nation in reaching and surpassing...

The post Star Power: Satellites in Space Boost Connectivity in Louisiana appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] The Revenue Stabilization Trust Fund: A Savings Account or a Spending Account?
item.description:
Louisiana’s reserve funds are designed to protect taxpayers during economic downturns and emergencies. But recent spending decisions raise important questions about whether state savings are being used as intended. Responsible budgeting means protecting Louisiana’s long-term fiscal stability and keeping rainy day funds ready for when they’re truly needed.

The post The Revenue Stabilization Trust Fund: A Savings Account or a Spending Account? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: Data Center Protests Are Hurting Main Street
item.description:
Data center opposition has become a national trend, uniting Americans across the country and across the political spectrum....

The post Florida Politics: Data Center Protests Are Hurting Main Street appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Task Forces, Studies, and Taxpayer Dollars: Is Louisiana Getting a Return on Investment?
item.description:
The 2026 Louisiana Legislative Session saw the creation of well over 100 task forces and agency-directed studies, raising concerns about the significant investment of taxpayer-funded time and resources that they require. Lawmakers approved each other’s formal requests for reports on everything from education funding and energy policy to healthcare and regulatory matters. When all was...

The post Task Forces, Studies, and Taxpayer Dollars: Is Louisiana Getting a Return on Investment? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Institute Defends Rights of Home-Sharers in Texas
item.description:
The Goldwater Institute today filed a brief in the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals urging the judges to reconsider a..

The post Goldwater Institute Defends Rights of Home-Sharers in Texas appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] New Census Bureau Data Show Northeast is an Outlier on New Housing Characteristics
item.description:
This month's edition of The House Call breaks down results from the Census Bureau's 2025 Characteristics of New Housing Survey, including affordability, amenities, and production. It also describes the Massachusetts court case that stopped a rent control petition from appearing before voters this fall.

The post New Census Bureau Data Show Northeast is an Outlier on New Housing Characteristics appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Property Does Not Exist to Support Government
item.description:
Supreme Court Clarifies Tax Sale Rights: In Pung v. Isabella County, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that taxpayers are entitled to the surplus proceeds from a tax sale—but not the fair market value of their property if it later sells for more. “Nothing Less, and Nothing More”: Building on its 2023 Tyler decision, the Court reaffirmed that governments may collect unpaid taxes through a tax sale, but may retain only what is owed and must return any surplus to the former property owner. Property Rights Still Matter: While concurring in the judgment, Justice Clarence Thomas emphasized that property rights are fundamental and argued that the Constitution places strict limits on the government’s power to take private property, reminding officials that “the government exists to protect property; property does not exist to support the government.”

The post Property Does Not Exist to Support Government appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Will Law] Citizens Defending Liberty v. Evers
item.description:
WILL filed a new lawsuit challenging the Wisconsin Supplier Diversity Program, a procurement program that spends over $250 million each year purchasing supplies and services based on race. White, Middle Eastern, and most Asian small-business owners are ineligible for this program simply because of their race.

The post Citizens Defending Liberty v. Evers appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] California AG Rob Bonta’s War on Affordability
item.description:
Critics of the policies that have turned California into the most inhospitable place in America to do business or try to make a living tend to focus on outgoing Governor Gavin Newsom. But California is a one-party state. Other entrenched politicians play a powerful supporting role, and Attorney General Rob Bonta is a prime example. Appointed...

The post California AG Rob Bonta’s War on Affordability first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] The silence breaks: Accountability at last for those who make Milwaukee life unlivable
item.description:
“The days of white liberal guilt are coming to an end and thank God for it.”

The post The silence breaks: Accountability at last for those who make Milwaukee life unlivable appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Why Congress’s Housing Act is Just Political Theater A recent...

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] How to strengthen the climb from the safety net
item.description:
Wisconsin’s next governor and Legislature should strengthen the state’s safety net by focusing it more clearly on work, family stability and upward mobility.

The post How to strengthen the climb from the safety net appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Author Julie Young to Discuss Say YES! How Virtual Became Reality in Live Webinar
item.description:
BOSTON — Julie Young, the founding CEO of Florida Virtual School, ASU Prep Digital and ASU Prep Global and one of the architects of the modern virtual learning movement has released a new book, Say YES! How Virtual Became Reality. Published in partnership with Pioneer Institute, the book chronicles the mindset shifts and disciplined practices that allowed her and her team to turn a small, grant-funded pilot into a first-of-its-kind national model serving millions of students globally. Young will discuss her journey on Monday, July 13 from 3:00 to 4:00 p.m. ET, in a live webinar hosted by edWeb.net.

The post Pioneer Institute Author Julie Young to Discuss Say YES! How Virtual Became Reality in Live Webinar appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] "Becoming Big Brother": Alcohol Ordinance Proposal Sparks Backlash In Idaho Falls
item.description: An Idaho Falls councilman has sounded the alarm over a proposed alcohol ordinance and expressed concerns over how it could jeopardize private property rights.   Councilman John Radford posted on Facebook Wednesday that the city council will vote on the alcohol ordinance Thursday, July 9. The meeting is slated to start at 6:30 p.m., and it […]

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Affordable housing buyers need immediate property tax exemption
item.description:
July 8, 2026, 10 a.m. Honolulu Hale To: Honolulu City Council Tommy Waters, Chair Andria Tupola, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Ted Kefalas, Director of Strategic Campaigns RE: Bill 40 (2026), CD1 — RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION Aloha Chair Waters, Vice Chair Tupola and other members of the Council, The Grassroot Institute […]

item.description:

The post Empty homes tax proposal is vague, legally risky, and could raise prices appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Chad Aldeman, education policy researcher and founder of Read Not Guess, about Missouri’s early literacy crisis and why the legislature has struggled to address it. They […]

item.description: In June, Yankee Institute proposed ERIN’s Act, a legislative framework to make state government purchasing-card transactions and receipts publicly searchable through a centralized online portal. The idea is simple: make […]The post ERIN’s Act Doesn’t Need to Cost Towns a Dime   appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] The Senior Services Tax Proposal in St. Louis County Is as Bad as It Gets
item.description:
If you sat down and intentionally tried to come up with the worst tax policy you possibly could, you would want to include the following elements: Raising taxes on some […]

The post The Senior Services Tax Proposal in St. Louis County Is as Bad as It Gets appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Rob Sand and Zach Lahn Outline Tax Agendas
item.description:
Iowa’s gubernatorial candidates recently outlined their tax philosophies during separate interviews on Simon Conway's WHO Radio program, offering some of the clearest policy positions of the campaign so far. Rob Sand discussed legalizing and taxing recreational cannabis and ending the sales tax exemption for data center equipment to generate additional state revenue, while maintaining he did not believe higher taxes would be necessary. Zach Lahn pledged not to raise Iowa's sales, income, or property taxes and said he would like to eventually eliminate the state income tax by reducing government waste and improving efficiency.

The post Rob Sand and Zach Lahn Outline Tax Agendas appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Statement of Pioneer Institute on ROAD Housing Act
item.description:
"As the federal ROAD to Housing Act becomes law tonight, it marks the most comprehensive supply-focused federal housing legislation in decades. Pioneer Institute applauds the Act’s focus on spurring housing production and ultimately improving housing affordability nationwide.

The post Statement of Pioneer Institute on ROAD Housing Act appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Defends State Constitutional Amendment to Block Discriminatory DEI
item.description:
Arizona voters will have a chance to banish discriminatory “diversity, equity, and inclusion” practices from the state thanks to a proposed state..

The post Goldwater Defends State Constitutional Amendment to Block Discriminatory DEI appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] In New England, low-tax New Hampshire has smallest percentage of residents who want to leave
item.description:
Granite Staters are told often that our low taxes are a liability and that copying the other New England state’s higher-tax, higher-spending model would improve affordability and the overall quality of life. New polling from the University of New Hampshire Survey Center suggests that the exact opposite is true. A series of UNH surveys released […]

The post In New England, low-tax New Hampshire has smallest percentage of residents who want to leave appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho and Montana are winning the competition for America’s families
item.description: Every year, millions of Americans make one of the biggest decisions of their lives: whether to stay where they are or move somewhere they believe offers greater opportunity. They aren’t just choosing a new home. They’re choosing a different set of public policies.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Liberty in Action: Yes, Pencils and Books Are Essential!
item.description:
No one doubts that pencils and books are essential school supplies—that’s why it was so ridiculous that Arizona Attorney General..

The post Liberty in Action: Yes, Pencils and Books Are Essential! appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Ninth Circuit Hears CPC’s First Amendment Challenge to SB 399
item.description:
On Tuesday, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit heard oral arguments in California Policy Center v. Garcia-Brower, CPC’s lawsuit challenging California Senate Bill 399, a law that violates employers’ First Amendment rights by prohibiting the discussion of “religious or political matters” during mandatory workplace meetings. Jeffrey Schwab, Senior Counsel with Liberty Justice Center, presented...

The post Ninth Circuit Hears CPC’s First Amendment Challenge to SB 399 first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Gaski: A Filibuster Primer
item.description: by John F. Gaski, Ph.D. A prominent political dilemma for Republicans is whether to abolish the Senate filibuster rule. Do so, and legislation will no longer require 60 votes for passage, only a simple majority. Doing so would also abandon a deliberative tradition in force since the mid-1800s. However, there presently is an overhang of another vital concern, Read the full article...

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Planting the seeds for progress
item.description:
Like many local business owners, Hawaii’s farmers and ranchers have to deal with rules and regulations that often make it harder to run a successful enterprise. They also share the same concerns about the high cost of housing and how it affects their ability to attract and retain employees. This year, with encouragement from my […]

The post Planting the seeds for progress appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Summer Parades
item.description: by Mark Franke It’s officially high summer according to the thermometer and, most importantly, according to the school vacation calendar. In the past that meant lots of community parades on Saturdays, most of which were aligned with that town’s summer festival week. Most of Fort Wayne’s  surrounding towns had them. I know, because my Navy Read the full article...

item.title: [Mackinac] Budget highlights and lowlights
item.description: New budget’s increase is not sustainable

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Missed opportunities: What to learn from Washington’s AI Task Force
item.description: Washington's AI Task Force failed to meaningfully help the state move the dial on leveraging AI to improve human flourishing. This outcome was in many ways predictable.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Before You Vote, Ask Kansas Politicians This One Question
item.description: Between now and the August 4 primary election, Kansans will hear familiar promises before they vote. Candidates will pledge lower taxes, better schools, safer communities, stronger healthcare, and a more affordable Kansas. Advance voting begins July 15, so those conversations are already taking place across the state. Listen carefully. Nearly every candidate will explain why […]

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Trump’s Push for Healthcare Transparency, Starting With Hospital Prices, Can Go Even Further
item.description:
The Trump administration is making hospital price transparency a centerpiece of its Make America Healthy Again agenda — and it has a chance to go even further toward achieving this goal. Earlier this month, federal regulators warned more than 500 hospitals that they could be violating federal requirements to disclose their prices. The message from […]

The post Trump’s Push for Healthcare Transparency, Starting With Hospital Prices, Can Go Even Further appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Case Study: Papillion and Missing Middle Housing
item.description:
This is the first in a series of “Research Notes” compiled by our summer intern, Ethan Roth. We have him looking at an assortment of things related to housing in Nebraska. We thought others might be interested in some of the things that he is seeing. These findings will add to our base of knowledge...

The post Case Study: Papillion and Missing Middle Housing appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Watch: Learn How California’s New Reforms Are Making it Easier to Build ADUs
item.description:
Watch below as a Sacramento-area homeowner gives us a tour of her family’s newly-build ADU and learn how California’s new reforms are making it much easier to build new ADUs.

The post Watch: Learn How California’s New Reforms Are Making it Easier to Build ADUs appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] PRESS RELEASE: ABORTION INITIATIVE IS ON THE NOVEMBER BALLOT
item.description: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE BOISE, Idaho–Today, Idaho Freedom Foundation President Ron Nate released the following statement after a radical abortion initiative qualified for Idaho’s November ballot: "There is no issue more important to me, or to Idaho Freedom Foundation, than the sanctity of life. Every right, privilege, and duty of citizenship flows from our most fundamental […]

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Opinion piece: Rebates now, tax reform later
item.description:
The following appeared in Las Cruces Sun News on July 11, 2027 Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham is to be applauded for calling for rebates for hard-pressed New Mexicans. With an additional $850 million available to the State due to elevated oil and gas prices because of the Iran War, New Mexicans should receive some help […]

The post RGF Opinion piece: Rebates now, tax reform later appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Stop the Toll Hike
item.description:
Illinois drivers already pay some of the highest taxes and fees in the country.

The post Stop the Toll Hike appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] A U.S. Attorney’s Perspective on Criminal Justice Reform with Thomas C. Albus
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Thomas C. Albus, United States Attorney for the Eastern District of Missouri, about public safety and criminal justice reform in the St. Louis region. They discuss […]

The post A U.S. Attorney’s Perspective on Criminal Justice Reform with Thomas C. Albus appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Spanberger makes questionable appointment to prison reform group
item.description:
Jessica Schwalb writes for the Washington Free Beacon about the latest evidence that Virginia’s governor is no moderate. A cabinet official “moderate” Virginia governor Abigail Spanberger (D.) tapped to co-lead a prison reform council headed the FBI field office in Richmond that issued an infamous, since-retracted Biden-era memo identifying traditionalist Catholics as potential domestic terrorists....

The post Spanberger makes questionable appointment to prison reform group appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Teddy Roosevelt library sends bad message about America
item.description:
Chris Bray writes for the Federalist about a disappointing presidential library. From a distance, it looked extremely promising, designed to blend in with a magnificent American landscape in what appeared to be a declaration of moral modesty and ideological restraint: a promise to focus on America. … … When you look back from the land...

The post Teddy Roosevelt library sends bad message about America appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Socialists take over America’s Democratic Party
item.description:
Arthur Schaper writes for American Greatness about a disturbing development in American politics. For decades, the Democratic Party exploited—but also restrained—its more liberal, anti-American activists. However, as they have become outrageously progressive, communistic, and violent, they have lost control of themselves, and now they are taking over. Democratic Party operatives thought they could maintain control,...

The post Socialists take over America’s Democratic Party appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] From the Summer of Love to the Summer of Likes
item.description:
A few years ago, a flash mob meant gathering in a public place to dance, or engage in some harmless act of collective absurdity. The goal was novelty. The participants sought attention, but not victims. In May, approximately 200 teenagers gathered at The Pike in Long Beach after a meetup circulated through social media. The […]

The post From the Summer of Love to the Summer of Likes appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois teacher pensions 7th-worst funded in U.S.
item.description:
Despite record high contributions from the state, the Teachers’ Retirement System has less than 50 cents on hand for every dollar owed.

The post Illinois teacher pensions 7th-worst funded in U.S. appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Chicago Teachers Union likes Brandon Johnson’s tax on sites like LinkedIn, TikTok
item.description:
The social media tax in the city’s 2026 budget already faces a lawsuit.

The post Chicago Teachers Union likes Brandon Johnson’s tax on sites like LinkedIn, TikTok appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Democrats’ Maine mess puts insiders back in charge
item.description:
The Graham Platner debacle raises a larger election-integrity question: Do voters still decide, or do parties reclaim power when elections get messy? “Inclusive.” “Representative.” “Transparent.” That is how Maine Democratic Party Chair Charlie Dingman describes the process for selecting a new Democratic nominee following Graham Platner’s withdrawal from the race. Those are worthy goals. However,...

The post Democrats’ Maine mess puts insiders back in charge first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] There’s less pork in the state budget but it’s still bad
item.description: $180 million in the 2026-27 budget

item.title: [Mackinac] Illicit smokes: 17% of cigarettes consumed in Michigan during 2024 were smuggled
item.description: New York regains top smuggling spot from California

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Indiana’s Muslim Test THE NEED to confront the Muslim scourge in our cities is dividing the Republican Party — or so you would expect. In Indiana, however, only Lt. Gov. Micah Beckwith is speaking out while other GOP leaders sit silent for fear that Julia Carson’s grandson, Andre, will call them bigots. Indeed, the governor upbraided Beckwith for not choosing his words more carefully, Read the full article...

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Don’t Fence Me In: Rural Ranchers Face Mounting Challenges
item.description:
Editor’s Note: This is the latest part of a series on rural Texas and the challenges that rural Texans face. Over the next year, the Associated News Service will delve into the issues facing the Lone Star State’s 177 (out of 254) rural counties. SULPHUR SPRINGS, Texas — Katie Koon says she didn’t stand a...

The post Don’t Fence Me In: Rural Ranchers Face Mounting Challenges first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Telling Renters the Truth about Property Taxes
item.description:
Renters oftentimes do not know how much in property taxes they pay monthly or annually. This knowledge gap can invite negative societal effects, such as uninformed voting decisions. Key points: Many renters are uninformed about their property tax burden. Some estimates suggest that as much as 20% of an individual’s rent is due to local...

The post Telling Renters the Truth about Property Taxes first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] WSU/KU professors’ budget model for USD 259 is misleading
item.description: Discussions about balancing government budgets inevitably prompt elected officials to ask taxpayers, “What service would you cut?” Regardless of intent, that tactic implies that government spending cannot be reduced without cutting services, and that seems to be the purpose of a public opinion effort on the USD 259 Wichita budget. Some taxpayers living in the […]

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Understanding the Supreme Court’s biggest decisions in 2026 | Dr. Matthew Brogdon & Bill Duncan
item.description:
Brogdon and Duncan explain why many popular interpretations you see online often fall short, and how we should be thinking about these cases instead.

The post Understanding the Supreme Court’s biggest decisions in 2026 | Dr. Matthew Brogdon & Bill Duncan appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WI Appeals Court Rules in Favor of Property Owners in Vacation Rental Dispute
item.description:
WILL secured a major legal victory for property owners in Sister Bay after the Wisconsin Court of Appeals ruled that the Village's four-bedroom cap for vacation rentals operates as an illegal "back-door prohibition on renting."

The post WI Appeals Court Rules in Favor of Property Owners in Vacation Rental Dispute appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Education policy needs less symbolic action, more real impact
item.description:
The gritty work of education policy, which has real impacts on students, comes from states or from federal policies that expand the work that states are trying to accomplish. That’s why attempts by the current administration to reduce federal bureaucracy in favor of state policies that affect real students are worthy of our support and attempts to file symbolic articles of impeachment are not.

The post Education policy needs less symbolic action, more real impact appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Electricity is Unaffordable; that was the plan, and the numbers confirm it
item.description:
“Affordability” has become a political buzz word in recent times, starting with Democrats in November 2025 and then later adopted by Republicans by at least March of this year. As Democrat pollster Geoff Garin put it: “The word’s important, because it speaks to the real-world challenge that Americans feel — it’s hard for them to...

The post Electricity is Unaffordable; that was the plan, and the numbers confirm it appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Audit shows lawmakers leading out on Utah Fits All Scholarship policy reforms
item.description:
Despite the attention some of the audit expenses have received, the real news is that many of these concerns have already been addressed and rooted out.

The post Audit shows lawmakers leading out on Utah Fits All Scholarship policy reforms appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Ratchet Only Goes One Way: Boise’s Ever Increasing Property Taxes
item.description: A brief history of Boise property taxes reveals only one trend: they are increasing. Property taxes have increased every fiscal year since FY21. The only reason FY21 was spared was due to pandemic-era funding from state and federal sources. In FY22, the base increase was 3%, in FY23 it was 2.45%, and in FY24 it […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] 65% of Minnesota 11th graders below grade level in math
item.description:
Nearly two-thirds of Minnesota 11th graders are below grade level in math, and the class of 2025 posted the worst academic performance on record for a graduating cohort even as it earned the highest graduation rate ever. As measured by the state’s Minnesota Comprehensive Assessment, the proficiency results reflect the percentage of juniors meeting the...

The post 65% of Minnesota 11th graders below grade level in math appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] American Experiment adds voice to national discussion on criminal justice
item.description:
Recent advocacy around solving non-fatal shootings in Minnesota led to my invitation to paticipate in this week’s Niskanen Center Third Annual Criminal Justice Convening in Savannah Georgia. The event brought together nearly 70 research, advocacy, and policy experts from across the nation to explore the emerging interventions that many believe will help strengthen our criminal...

The post American Experiment adds voice to national discussion on criminal justice appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] New study: Montana should join the flat tax revolution
item.description: Montana has become one of America's economic success stories, but a new study released today by the Mountain States Policy Center argues the Treasure State risks falling behind unless it continues reforming its tax code.
